relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul and Sosthenes open from calling, not self-importance.
Corinth is called holy together with all who call on Jesus’ name.
Grace and peace begin the healing.
Paul gives thanks for grace already given.
The community is enriched in word and knowledge.
Messiah’s witness has been confirmed among them.
They lack no grace-gift while awaiting unveiling.
The Lord confirms them toward the end.
The trustworthy Divine calls them into participation with the Son.
The first wound named is split; the medicine is restored mind and discernment.
Chloe’s people reveal the strife honestly.
Faction-names replace shared belonging.
Paul asks whether Messiah can be divided.
He refuses to become the center of anyone’s baptismal identity.
No one was baptized into Paul’s name.
Stephanas’ household is remembered without building a faction.
Good news must not empty the cross through status-wisdom.
The cross-word appears foolish to the perishing but power to the saved/healed.
Divine wisdom interrupts the wisdom that props up pride.
The age’s experts are questioned before Divine reversal.
The Divine saves through proclamation that humbles status-wisdom.
Different groups demand signs or wisdom.
Crucified Messiah disrupts both sign-demand and wisdom-demand.
To the called, Messiah is Divine power and Divine wisdom.
Divine foolishness and weakness overturn human measures.
Their calling did not mostly come through worldly rank.
The Divine chooses what the world dismisses to shame status-power.
The nothings are chosen to make inactive the somethings.
No flesh gets to boast before the Divine.
Messiah becomes wisdom, justice, sanctification, and release.
Boasting returns to the Lord alone.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 1 begins community healing by breaking faction-identity and status-wisdom. The cross becomes the medicine that makes boasting collapse.
